stemHtml: What is the working principle of a gaseous fire detection system?

options.a: A closed tube, when heated, causes the gas inside to expand and trigger an alarm via a pressure switch.
options.b: A closed tube consists of a gas which changes its translucence when heated. Light sensors will sense such change in translucence and trigger an alarm.
options.c: A closed tube, when heated, causes the gas inside to contract which causes a negative pressure and thereby trigger an alarm via a pressure switch.
options.d: A closed tube, when heated, causes the gas inside to become a conductor and thereby triggers an alarm circuit.
